channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: NHSE tech funding priorities revealed
item.description: Regions are prioritising investing in ambient voice technology and cyber security over other technology this year, HSJ understands.

item.title: US equity firm buys biggest supplier of GP IT
item.description: An American private equity firm has bought the company supplying more than half of England’s primary care IT systems.

item.title: ‘Concerning’ levels of therapy still carried out by unaccredited staff
item.description: Tens of thousands of therapy sessions are still being carried out by unaccredited practitioners in the NHS, data suggests – nearly four years after the deadline to stamp this out.

item.title: Streeting intervenes over energy price shock
item.description: The health secretary has urged hospital chief executives to sign up to a national energy procurement scheme to cushion themselves from energy price shocks caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Failing private service faces NHS takeover
item.description: A leading NHS provider is considering taking over the running of services at a private hospital at the centre of a police probe.

item.title: Exclusive: No 10 sets new GP access target
item.description: A new target for improving patients’ experience of making GP appointments is among three top NHS priorities identified by the prime minister for this year, HSJ has learned.

item.title: NHSE locks down ‘open source’ code over Mythos AI fears
item.description: NHS England is restricting access to open source code after researchers found that the Mythos AI model could expose “pretty severe” vulnerabilities in commonly used software.

item.title: Mackey’s heir named number one trust CEO
item.description: The top two trust chief executives in the 12th edition of HSJ’s annual ranking of the most respected provider CEOs have taken very different routes to the summit.

item.title: French tech giant seeks to crack GP IT duopoly
item.description: An insurgent NHS tech supplier is planning a major expansion after being bought out by a European company.

item.title: Ex-civil servant made trust CEO
item.description: Cambridge University Hospitals Foundation Trust has appointed Nicola Ayton as its new permanent chief executive, the provider has announced.

item.title: Trusts see surge in AI-generated complaints
item.description: Trusts’ complaints teams are facing a wave of AI-generated complaints letters which can run to dozens of pages, deploying inaccurate legal arguments and containing hallucinated information, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Leaked review warns CDCs a ‘burden’ on trusts
item.description: Community diagnostic centres could become a financial “burden” on providers without extra funding and changes to how tests are paid for, the programme’s architect has warned in an internal review obtained by HSJ.

item.title: Ex-NHSE director joins NICE top team
item.description: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence has appointed a GP, and former NHS England national clinical director, as its new chief medical officer.

item.title: GPs to withhold patient data in contract dispute
item.description: GP practices should take “collective action” in protest against their contract by cutting back on sharing patient data, the British Medical Association’s GP committee has said.

item.title: Trust criticised over child death
item.description: The care of a five-year-old boy who died at a specialist hospital “did not meet the standards expected”, an external review has said.

item.title: Trusts ‘really poor’ at IT rollouts, Mackey warns
item.description: The rollout of IT systems has created a “degree of turmoil” until recently, the chief executive of NHS England has admitted.

item.title: ‘Influential’ CEO quits leading provider to head new trust
item.description: A chief executive who lost accountable officer status when her trust adopted a group model last month is set to leave to run another acute provider.

item.title: Advice and guidance ‘adding to backlogs’, say consultants
item.description: Many medical consultants report a “mixed” experience with the advice and guidance model, saying it is “under-resourced and adding to existing backlogs”, according to research by an integrated care board.

item.title: 3,000 staff exiting NHSE
item.description: More than 700 staff have left NHS England this year through voluntary exit and redundancy schemes and another 2,300 are due to depart by next April, national leaders have said.

item.title: New CEO role for ex-crisis trust boss
item.description: Oxford Health Foundation Trust has appointed its next chief executive, who joins from a recently dissolved mental health trust.
